title: Infiltrator
description: Exploit a PHP LFI vulnerability to read sensitive system files and escalate to Remote Code Execution through log poisoning.
longDescription:
A PHP web application uses a ?page= parameter to include template files — without sanitizing path traversal sequences. The vulnerability is a textbook Local File Inclusion.

Start by reading /etc/passwd to confirm the vulnerability, then escalate to Remote Code Execution via Apache log poisoning to get a shell and read the flag.

### What you will practice
Path traversal, PHP wrappers (php://filter), log poisoning via User-Agent injection, and chaining LFI to RCE.
creator: Dock & Root Labs
objectives: Identify the LFI vulnerability in the page parameter
Poison the Apache access log with PHP code via User-Agent
Trigger log inclusion to execute code and read the flag
